q: Which of the following is a classic example of a 'Papulosquamous' disease? / निम्नलिखित में से कौन सा 'पैप्युलोस्क्वैमस' रोग का एक क्लासिक उदाहरण है?
e: Papulosquamous diseases like Psoriasis and Lichen Planus are characterized by papules and scales.

q: What is the hallmark clinical feature of 'Vesiculo-bullous' disorders? / 'वेसिकुलो-बुलस' विकारों की प्रमुख नैदानिक विशेषता क्या है?
o: Formation of blisters and bullae | फफोले और छाले बनना
e: These disorders, such as Pemphigus, involve the formation of fluid-filled lesions within or below the epidermis.

q: Which condition is a disorder of 'Melanocytes' leading to white patches? / मेलानोसाइट्स का कौन सा विकार सफेद धब्बों का कारण बनता है?
e: Vitiligo is an autoimmune destruction of melanocytes resulting in depigmented skin patches.

q: What is the priority intervention for a child in 'Status Epilepticus'? / 'स्टेटस एपिलेप्टिकस' में बच्चे के लिए प्राथमिकता हस्तक्षेप क्या है?
o: Ensuring airway and giving Benzodiazepines (e.g., Diazepam) | एयरवे और दवा
e: Status epilepticus is a medical emergency; maintaining a clear airway and stopping the seizure is vital.

q: Which sign indicates 'Severe Dehydration' in a child with diarrhea? / दस्त वाले बच्चे में 'गंभीर निर्जलीकरण' का संकेत क्या है?
o: Sunken eyes and delayed skin pinch return | धँसी हुई आँखें
e: Severe dehydration shows clinical signs like loss of skin turgor, sunken fontanelle, and lethargy.

q: What is 'ADHD' characterized by? / 'ADHD' की विशेषता क्या है?
o: Inattention, hyperactivity, and impulsivity | ध्यान की कमी और अतिसक्रियता
e: Attention-Deficit/Hyperactivity Disorder affects a child's ability to focus and control behavior.

q: Which infection is suspected when a child has 'Koplik spots' in the mouth? / जब बच्चे के मुँह में 'कोप्लिक स्पॉट्स' होते हैं तो किस संक्रमण का संदेह होता है?
e: Koplik spots are small white spots inside the cheek, a prodromal sign of Measles.

q: What is the hallmark of 'Shock' in a pediatric patient? / पीडियाट्रिक मरीज में 'शॉक' की प्रमुख विशेषता क्या है?
o: Inadequate tissue perfusion (Low BP and cold limbs) | ऊतक छिड़काव में कमी
e: Shock leads to poor oxygen delivery to tissues, often presenting with fast heart rate and weak pulses.

q: In pediatric basic life support, what is the 'Compression to Breath' ratio for a single rescuer? / बाल चिकित्सा बीएलएस में, एकल बचावकर्ता के लिए 'संपीड़न से सांस' का अनुपात क्या है?
e: For a single rescuer, 30 chest compressions followed by 2 breaths is the standard ratio.

q: What is 'Pertussis' also known as? / 'पर्टुसिस' (Pertussis) को और किस नाम से जाना जाता है?
e: It is a highly contagious respiratory infection characterized by a severe 'whooping' cough.

q: Which behavior is a typical sign of 'Separation Anxiety Disorder'? / 'पृथक्करण चिंता विकार' का विशिष्ट लक्षण क्या है?
o: Excessive distress when away from home or caregivers | देखभाल करने वालों से दूर होने पर तनाव
e: It is normal at a certain age but becomes a disorder if it is excessive and interferes with daily life.

q: What is the emergency management for a 'Foreign Body Aspiration' (choking) in an infant? / एक शिशु में 'विदेशी वस्तु एस्पिरेशन' (दम घुटना) के लिए आपातकालीन प्रबंधन क्या है?
o: Back blows and chest thrusts | बैक ब्लो और चेस्ट थ्रस्ट
e: Abdominal thrusts (Heimlich) are not used in infants; instead, back slaps and chest compressions are performed.

q: Which electrolyte imbalance is common in a child with severe vomiting? / गंभीर उल्टी वाले बच्चे में कौन सा इलेक्ट्रोलाइट असंतुलन आम है?
e: Loss of stomach acid (HCl) and fluids leads to low potassium and high blood pH.

q: Which sign is a clinical indicator of 'Pyloric Stenosis' in an infant? / एक शिशु में 'पायलोरिक स्टेनोसिस' का नैदानिक संकेतक कौन सा है?
o: Projectile vomiting and olive-shaped mass | प्रोजेक्टाइल उल्टी
e: Hypertrophic pyloric stenosis causes forceful vomiting and a palpable mass in the upper abdomen.

q: What is the most common childhood 'Malignancy'? / बच्चों में सबसे आम 'घातक रोग' (Malignancy) कौन सा है?
e: ALL is the most frequent cancer in children, affecting white blood cells in the bone marrow.

q: Which condition is characterized by 'painless hematuria' and a large abdominal mass in children? / बच्चों में 'दर्द रहित हेमट्यूरिया' और पेट में बड़े द्रव्यमान की विशेषता क्या है?
e: Wilms tumor is a common kidney cancer in young children that usually presents as a firm, non-tender mass.

q: What is the hallmark of 'Cystic Fibrosis' in the gastrointestinal system? / गैस्ट्रोइंटेस्टाइनल सिस्टम में 'सिस्टिक फाइब्रोसिस' की प्रमुख विशेषता क्या है?
e: Thick mucus blocks pancreatic ducts, leading to malabsorption of fats and smelly, greasy stools.

q: Which type of 'Anemia' is caused by the premature destruction of RBCs? / आरबीसी के समय से पहले विनाश के कारण कौन सा 'एनीमिया' होता है?
e: Hemolytic anemia occurs when the rate of RBC destruction exceeds the rate of production.

q: What is 'Cerebral Palsy' primarily? / 'सेरेब्रल पाल्सी' (Cerebral Palsy) मुख्य रूप से क्या है?
o: A group of permanent disorders of movement and posture | स्थायी आंदोलन विकार
e: It is caused by non-progressive damage to the developing brain before or during birth.

q: Which hormone is deficient in 'Diabetes Insipidus'? / 'डायबिटीज इंसिपिडस' में किस हार्मोन की कमी होती है?
e: Lack of ADH leads to the inability of kidneys to conserve water, causing excessive urination and thirst.

q: What is 'Talipes Equinovarus' commonly known as? / 'टैलिप्स इक्विनोवेरस' को आमतौर पर किस नाम से जाना जाता है?
e: Clubfoot is a congenital deformity where the infant's foot is turned inward and downward.

q: The 'Sweat Chloride Test' is the diagnostic gold standard for: / 'स्वेट क्लोराइड टेस्ट' किसके लिए डायग्नोस्टिक गोल्ड स्टैंडर्ड है?
e: High levels of chloride in sweat are a definitive indicator of Cystic Fibrosis.

q: What is 'Thalassemia'? / 'थैलेसीमिया' (Thalassemia) क्या है?
o: Genetic disorder of hemoglobin synthesis | हीमोग्लोबिन संश्लेषण का आनुवंशिक विकार
e: It leads to the production of abnormal hemoglobin, causing chronic anemia and requiring frequent transfusions.

q: What is the most common cause of 'Acute Diarrhea' in children? / बच्चों में 'तीव्र दस्त' का सबसे आम कारण क्या है?
e: Rotavirus is the leading cause of severe dehydrating diarrhea in infants and young children worldwide.

q: What is 'Intussusception'? / 'इंटुसुसेप्शन' (Intussusception) क्या है?
o: Telescoping of one part of the intestine into another | आंत का एक हिस्सा दूसरे में समा जाना
e: It is a medical emergency that can cause bowel obstruction and is common in infants.

q: Which clinical sign is characteristic of 'Nephrotic Syndrome' in children? / बच्चों में 'नेफ्रोटिक सिंड्रोम' का विशिष्ट नैदानिक लक्षण क्या है?
o: Generalized edema (Puffiness) and Proteinuria | सूजन और प्रोटीनुरिया
e: Heavy loss of protein in urine leads to fluid shifts and significant swelling, often starting around the eyes.

q: What is the 'Gold Standard' for diagnosing 'Hirschsprung Disease'? / 'हिर्शsprung रोग' के निदान के लिए 'गोल्ड स्टैंडर्ड' क्या है?
e: Biopsy confirms the absence of ganglion cells in the distal bowel, which is the hallmark of the disease.

q: Which neurologic disorder involves 'recurrent unprovoked seizures'? / किस न्यूरोलॉजिकल विकार में 'बार-बार होने वाले अकारण दौरे' शामिल हैं?
e: Epilepsy is diagnosed when a child has repeated seizures not caused by an immediate trigger like fever.

q: What is the primary treatment for 'Febrile Seizures'? / 'फेब्राइल सीजर' (बुखार वाले दौरे) का प्राथमिक उपचार क्या है?
o: Controlling fever and parental reassurance | बुखार पर नियंत्रण
e: Most febrile seizures are benign and occur due to rapid rise in body temperature in young children.

q: Which sign is a characteristic of 'Autism Spectrum Disorder' (ASD)? / 'ऑटिज़्म स्पेक्ट्रम डिसऑर्डर' (ASD) की विशेषता क्या है?
o: Impaired social communication and repetitive behaviors | सामाजिक संचार में कमी
e: Children with ASD often have difficulty with social interactions and restricted, repetitive patterns of interest.

q: What is the focus of 'Neonatal Resuscitation'? / 'नवजात पुनर्जीवन' (Neonatal Resuscitation) का केंद्र क्या है?
o: Establishing effective ventilation | प्रभावी वेंटिलेशन स्थापित करना
e: In newborns, clearing the airway and supporting breathing is the most critical step in resuscitation.

q: What is 'Croup' (Laryngotracheobronchitis) characterized by? / 'क्रुप' (Croup) की विशेषता क्या है?
o: Barking cough and inspiratory stridor | भौंकने जैसी खांसी
e: It is a viral infection causing swelling around the vocal cords, leading to a distinctive cough.

q: Which emergency condition is suspected in a child with 'sudden onset high fever and drooling'? / 'अचानक तेज बुखार और लार टपकने' वाले बच्चे में किस आपातकालीन स्थिति का संदेह होता है?
e: Epiglottitis is a life-threatening airway emergency; drooling occurs because swallowing is too painful.

q: What is the 'Cephalocaudal' principle of growth? / वृद्धि का 'सेफालोकाउडल' (Cephalocaudal) सिद्धांत क्या है?
o: Growth from head to toe | सिर से पैर की ओर विकास
e: Development proceeds from the head downwards to the lower parts of the body.

q: What is the hallmark of 'Neonatal Respiratory Distress Syndrome' (RDS)? / 'नियोनेटल रेस्पिरेटरी डिस्ट्रेस सिंड्रोम' (RDS) की प्रमुख विशेषता क्या है?
o: Surfactant deficiency in preterm infants | सरफैक्टेंट की कमी
e: RDS occurs primarily in premature babies whose lungs haven't yet produced enough surfactant to stay open.

q: At what age does a child usually start 'social smiling'? / बच्चा आमतौर पर किस उम्र में 'सोशल स्माइलिंग' शुरू करता है?
e: Social smiling is a key developmental milestone occurring around 6-8 weeks of age.

q: What is the primary treatment for 'Phototherapy' in neonates? / नवजात शिशुओं में 'फोटोथेरेपी' का प्राथमिक उद्देश्य क्या है?
o: To reduce unconjugated bilirubin in Jaundice | पीलिया में बिलीरुबिन कम करना
e: Phototherapy helps break down bilirubin in the skin so it can be excreted, preventing brain damage (kernicterus).

q: Which vitamin is administered at birth to prevent 'Hemorrhagic disease of the newborn'? / नवजात के रक्तस्रावी रोग को रोकने के लिए जन्म के समय कौन सा विटामिन दिया जाता है?
e: Newborns have low Vitamin K stores; an injection is vital for blood clotting factors.

q: What is the 'APGAR' score used for? / 'APGAR' स्कोर का उपयोग किसके लिए किया जाता है?
o: Immediate clinical assessment of newborn at birth | जन्म के समय तत्काल मूल्यांकन
e: APGAR assesses heart rate, respiration, muscle tone, reflex irritability, and color at 1 and 5 minutes after birth.

q: Exclusive breastfeeding is recommended for the first: / विशेष स्तनपान की सिफारिश पहले कितने समय के लिए की जाती है?
e: WHO recommends only breast milk for the first 6 months for optimal growth and immunity.

q: What is the sign of 'Severe Acute Malnutrition' (SAM) in children? / बच्चों में 'गंभीर तीव्र कुपोषण' (SAM) का संकेत क्या है?
o: Mid-upper arm circumference (MUAC) <11.5 cm | एमयूएसी <11.5 सेमी
e: MUAC is a quick screening tool for malnutrition; a value below 11.5 cm indicates SAM.

q: Which congenital heart defect is most common in children? / बच्चों में सबसे आम जन्मजात हृदय दोष कौन सा है?
o: Ventricular Septal Defect (VSD) | वेंट्रिकुलर सेप्टल डिफेक्ट
e: VSD is a hole in the wall separating the two lower chambers of the heart.

q: What is 'Complementary Feeding'? / 'पूरक आहार' (Complementary Feeding) क्या है?
o: Introducing solid foods along with breast milk after 6 months | 6 महीने बाद ठोस आहार देना
e: It bridges the gap between the energy provided by breast milk and the child's increasing needs.

q: Which type of spinal fracture is commonly caused by vertical compression (e.g., falling from a height)? / ऊर्ध्वाधर संपीड़न (जैसे ऊंचाई से गिरना) के कारण आमतौर पर किस प्रकार का रीढ़ का फ्रैक्चर होता है?
e: Compression fractures often occur in the thoracic or lumbar vertebrae.

q: What is the primary objective of 'Spinal Fixation' surgery? / 'स्पाइनल फिक्सेशन' सर्जरी का प्राथमिक उद्देश्य क्या है?
o: To stabilize the spine and protect nerve roots | रीढ़ को स्थिर करना
e: Fixation uses rods, screws, or plates to hold vertebrae in place while they fuse together.

q: What is 'Cauda Equina Syndrome'? / 'कॉडा इक्विना सिंड्रोम' (Cauda Equina Syndrome) क्या है?
o: Compression of lower spinal nerve roots (Emergency) | निचली रीढ़ की नसों का दबना
e: It is a surgical emergency presenting with saddle anesthesia and loss of bowel/bladder control.

q: Which ligament injury is known as 'Skier's Thumb'? / किस लिगामेंट की चोट को 'स्कियर थंब' (Skier's Thumb) कहा जाता है?
o: Ulnar Collateral Ligament (UCL) of the thumb | अंगूठे का यूसीएल
e: It involves a tear of the UCL at the base of the thumb, often due to a fall while skiing.

q: What is the hallmark of 'Ankylosing Spondylitis' on X-ray? / एक्स-रे पर 'एंकिलॉजिंग स्पॉन्डिलाइटिस' की प्रमुख विशेषता क्या है?
e: Chronic inflammation leads to fusion of the vertebrae, giving it the appearance of a bamboo stalk.

q: Which surgery is performed for a 'Herniated Disc' to relieve nerve pressure? / तंत्रिका दबाव को कम करने के लिए 'हर्नियेटेड डिस्क' के लिए कौन सी सर्जरी की जाती है?
e: Discectomy involves removing the damaged part of a spinal disc that is pressing on a nerve.

q: What is 'Scoliosis'? / 'स्कोलोसिस' (Scoliosis) क्या है?
o: Abnormal lateral (side-to-side) curvature of the spine | रीढ़ का तिरछापन
e: Scoliosis is a sideways curve of the spine that often develops just before puberty.

q: Which fixation device is used for 'Intertrochanteric' femur fractures? / 'इंटरट्रोकेन्टरिक' फीमर फ्रैक्चर के लिए किस फिक्सेशन डिवाइस का उपयोग किया जाता है?
o: Dynamic Hip Screw (DHS) | डायनेमिक हिप स्क्रू
e: DHS allows for controlled collapse and compression at the fracture site, promoting healing.

q: What is 'Compartment Syndrome'? / 'कंपार्टमेंट सिंड्रोम' (Compartment Syndrome) क्या है?
o: Increased pressure within a muscle compartment (Emergency) | मांसपेशियों में बढ़ता दबाव
e: It can lead to muscle and nerve death if not treated immediately with a fasciotomy.

q: What is the primary treatment for 'Carpal Tunnel Syndrome'? / 'कार्पल टनल सिंड्रोम' का प्राथमिक उपचार क्या है?
o: Release of the transverse carpal ligament | कार्पल लिगामेंट रिलीज
e: Surgery aims to relieve pressure on the median nerve by cutting the ligament that forms the roof of the tunnel.

q: Which organism is the most common cause of 'Acute Pyogenic Osteomyelitis'? / 'एक्यूट पायोजेनिक ऑस्टियोमाइलाइटिस' का सबसे आम कारण कौन सा जीव है?
e: Staphylococcus aureus is responsible for the majority of bone infections in both children and adults.

q: What is 'Sequestrum' in the context of bone infection? / हड्डी के संक्रमण के संदर्भ में 'सेक्वेस्ट्रम' (Sequestrum) क्या है?
o: A piece of dead bone separated from living tissue | मृत हड्डी का टुकड़ा
e: Sequestrum is dead bone that acts as a reservoir for bacteria, often seen in chronic osteomyelitis.

q: Which primary bone tumor is characterized by 'Codman's Triangle' on radiology? / रेडियोलॉजी पर 'कोडमैन्स ट्रायंगल' किस प्राथमिक हड्डी के ट्यूमर की विशेषता है?
e: Codman's triangle is a type of periosteal reaction seen when a tumor lifts the periosteum away from the bone.

q: What is an 'Autograft' in bone grafting? / बोन ग्राफ्टिंग में 'ऑटोग्राफ्ट' (Autograft) क्या है?
o: Bone taken from the patient's own body | मरीज के अपने शरीर से ली गई हड्डी
e: Autografts are the gold standard because they provide living cells and pose no risk of rejection.

q: Which condition is a benign bone tumor often found in the epiphysis of long bones? / लंबी हड्डियों के एपिफिसिस में पाया जाने वाला सौम्य हड्डी का ट्यूमर कौन सा है?
o: Giant Cell Tumor (GCT) | जाइंट सेल ट्यूमर
e: GCT is locally aggressive but usually benign, often occurring around the knee joint.

q: What is the primary sign of 'Septic Arthritis'? / 'सेप्टिक अर्थराइटिस' का प्राथमिक संकेत क्या है?
o: Acute joint pain, swelling, and restricted movement | तीव्र जोड़ों का दर्द और सूजन
e: Septic arthritis is a medical emergency requiring immediate drainage to prevent joint destruction.

q: In fracture healing, what is 'Involucrum'? / फ्रैक्चर हीलिंग या संक्रमण में 'इनवोलुक्रम' (Involucrum) क्या है?
o: A layer of new bone growth around a sequestrum | मृत हड्डी के चारों ओर नई हड्डी
e: Involucrum is the body's attempt to form new bone around infected or dead bone tissue.

q: Which metabolic disease is known as 'The Silent Thief' of bones? / किस मेटाबॉलिक बीमारी को हड्डियों का 'साइलेंट थीफ' कहा जाता है?
e: Osteoporosis reduces bone density without symptoms until a fracture occurs.

q: What is an 'Allograft'? / 'एलोग्राफ्ट' (Allograft) क्या है?
o: Bone tissue from another human donor | दूसरे मानव दाता से ली गई हड्डी
e: Allografts are often used when the patient does not have enough bone available for an autograft.

q: Which site is most commonly used to harvest an 'Autograft'? / ऑटोग्राफ्ट प्राप्त करने के लिए सबसे अधिक किस स्थान का उपयोग किया जाता है?
e: The iliac crest provides a rich source of both cortical and cancellous bone.

q: What is 'Arthrodesis'? / 'आर्थ्रोडेसिस' (Arthrodesis) क्या है?
o: Surgical fusion of a joint | जोड़ का सर्जिकल संलयन (Fusion)
e: Arthrodesis is performed to eliminate pain by fusing two bones together, making the joint immobile.

q: Which ligament is most commonly injured in a 'Sports Injury' of the knee? / घुटने की 'स्पोर्ट्स इंजरी' में कौन सा लिगामेंट सबसे अधिक घायल होता है?
e: ACL tears are frequent in athletes involved in sports that require sudden stops or changes in direction.

q: What is the hallmark of 'Spondylolisthesis'? / 'स्पोंडिलोलिस्थीसिस' (Spondylolisthesis) की प्रमुख विशेषता क्या है?
o: Forward displacement of one vertebra over another | कशेरुका का आगे खिसकना
e: It typically occurs in the lower spine (lumbar region) and can cause back pain or nerve compression.

q: Which minimally invasive technique is used to see and treat joint problems? / जोड़ों की समस्याओं को देखने और इलाज करने के लिए किस मिनिमल इनवेसिव तकनीक का उपयोग किया जाता है?
e: Arthroscopy uses a small camera (arthroscope) to visualize and repair joint tissues through tiny incisions.

q: What is the primary risk of a 'Cervical Spine' injury? / 'सर्वाइकल स्पाइन' की चोट का प्राथमिक जोखिम क्या है?
o: Quadriplegia and respiratory failure | चतुर्भुज पक्षाघात (Quadriplegia)
e: Injury to the upper spine can paralyze all four limbs and affect the nerves controlling breathing.

q: What is a 'Bone Graft' used for? / 'बोन ग्राफ्ट' (Bone Graft) का उपयोग किसके लिए किया जाता है?
o: To promote bone healing in non-union or defects | हड्डी के उपचार को बढ़ावा देने के लिए
e: Bone grafting provides a scaffold and growth factors to help new bone grow across a gap or defect.

q: Which condition is a degenerative disorder of the spine involving the intervertebral disc? / इंटरवर्टेब्रल डिस्क से जुड़ा रीढ़ का अपक्षयी विकार कौन सा है?
o: Herniated Nucleus Pulposus (Disc Prolapse) | डिस्क प्रोलैप्स
e: Disc herniation occurs when the soft center of a spinal disc pushes through a crack in the tougher exterior.

q: What is 'Kyphosis'? / 'काइफोसिस' (Kyphosis) क्या है?
o: Abnormal outward curvature of the upper back (Hunchback) | कुबड़ापन
e: Kyphosis results in a rounded upper back, often seen in elderly patients with osteoporosis.

q: Which fixation method is 'External Fixation'? / कौन सी फिक्सेशन विधि 'एक्सटर्नल फिक्सेशन' (External Fixation) है?
o: Plates and screws inside the skin
A frame outside the limb connected by pins through the bone | बाहरी फ्रेम
e: External fixation is often used for complex open fractures where internal hardware cannot be placed immediately.

q: What is the 'Tennis Elbow' (Lateral Epicondylitis) a result of? / 'टेनिस एल्बो' (लैटरल एपिकोंडिलाइटिस) किसका परिणाम है?
o: Repetitive strain/overuse of forearm muscles | बार-बार होने वाला तनाव
e: It is a painful condition of the elbow caused by overuse of the muscles that extend the wrist.

q: Which metabolic bone disease is characterized by 'softening of bones' due to Vitamin D deficiency in adults? / वयस्कों में विटामिन D की कमी से हड्डियों के नरम होने वाले मेटाबॉलिक रोग को क्या कहते हैं?
e: Osteomalacia is the adult form of Vitamin D deficiency, whereas Rickets occurs in children.

q: What is the hallmark of 'Osteomyelitis'? / 'ऑस्टियोमाइलाइटिस' (Osteomyelitis) की प्रमुख विशेषता क्या है?
o: Infection of the bone and bone marrow | हड्डी और बोन मैरो का संक्रमण
e: Osteomyelitis is an inflammatory condition of the bone caused by an infectious organism, usually bacteria.

q: Which bone tumor shows a 'Sunburst' appearance on X-ray? / एक्स-रे पर कौन सा हड्डी का ट्यूमर 'सनबर्स्ट' (Sunburst) रूप दिखाता है?
e: Osteosarcoma is a highly malignant primary bone tumor often showing periosteal reaction known as sunburst appearance.

q: What is 'Malunion' of a fracture? / फ्रैक्चर का 'मलयूनियन' (Malunion) क्या है?
o: Healing of a fracture in an abnormal position | गलत स्थिति में हड्डी का जुड़ना
e: Malunion occurs when the bone pieces join back together but in a misaligned or bent way.

q: Which joint is most commonly affected by 'Osteoarthritis'? / 'ऑस्टियोआर्थराइटिस' से सबसे अधिक कौन सा जोड़ प्रभावित होता है?
e: Osteoarthritis is a degenerative joint disease frequently affecting weight-bearing joints like the knee.

q: What is 'Non-union' in fracture healing? / फ्रैक्चर हीलिंग में 'नॉन-यूनियन' (Non-union) क्या है?
o: Failure of bone ends to unite after 6-9 months | हड्डी के सिरों का न जुड़ना
e: Non-union is diagnosed when a fracture shows no signs of healing over a prolonged period.

q: What does 'Arthroplasty' refer to? / 'आर्थ्रोप्लास्टी' (Arthroplasty) का क्या तात्पर्य है?
o: Surgical reconstruction or replacement of a joint | जोड़ का प्रतिस्थापन
e: Total Hip or Knee Replacement is a common form of arthroplasty to restore joint function.

q: Which material is a common 'Biomaterial' used in orthopedic implants? / ऑर्थोपेडिक इम्प्लांट में इस्तेमाल होने वाला सामान्य 'बायोमटेरियल' कौन सा है?
o: Titanium and Stainless Steel | टाइटेनियम और स्टेनलेस स्टील
e: Biomaterials must be biocompatible, corrosion-resistant, and strong enough to support weight.

q: A 'Subluxation' is defined as: / 'सब्लक्सेशन' (Subluxation) को इस प्रकार परिभाषित किया गया है:
o: Complete loss of contact between joint surfaces
Partial dislocation of a joint | जोड़ का आंशिक विस्थापन
e: Subluxation involves a partial separation of joint surfaces, unlike a complete dislocation.

q: What is the primary sign of 'Gouty Arthritis' during an attack? / एक हमले के दौरान 'गाउटी अर्थराइटिस' का प्राथमिक लक्षण क्या है?
o: Sudden severe pain, redness, and swelling (often in big toe) | तीव्र दर्द और सूजन
e: Gout is caused by uric acid crystal deposits, most commonly in the first metatarsophalangeal joint.

q: What is the hallmark clinical feature of 'Glaucoma'? / 'ग्लूकोमा' की प्रमुख नैदानिक विशेषता क्या है?
o: Increased intraocular pressure and optic nerve damage | बढ़ा हुआ आईओपी
e: Glaucoma is a group of diseases that damage the optic nerve, often linked to high fluid pressure in the eye.

q: A 'Cataract' is defined as: / 'मोतियाबिंद' (Cataract) को इस प्रकार परिभाषित किया गया है:
o: Opacification of the natural crystalline lens | लेंस का धुंधला होना
e: Cataracts cause a progressive, painless decrease in vision due to lens clouding.

q: Which condition involves a 'cherry-red spot' on the macula? / मैकुला पर 'चेरी-रेड स्पॉट' किस स्थिति में दिखाई देता है?
o: Central Retinal Artery Occlusion (CRAO) | केंद्रीय रेटिनल धमनी रोधगलन
e: A cherry-red spot is a classic sign of CRAO, indicating an emergency lack of blood flow to the retina.

q: What is 'Strabismus'? / 'स्ट्रैबिस्मस' (Strabismus) क्या है?
o: Misalignment of the eyes (Squint) | आँखों का तिरछापन
e: Strabismus is a condition where the eyes do not look at the same point at the same time.

q: Which disease is characterized by 'drusen' deposits under the retina? / रेटिना के नीचे 'ड्रुसेन' (drusen) जमा होने की विशेषता क्या है?
e: Drusen are yellow deposits that are an early sign of dry macular degeneration.

q: What is 'Trachoma' caused by? / 'ट्रैकोमा' (Trachoma) किसके कारण होता है?
e: Trachoma is a bacterial infection and a leading cause of preventable blindness worldwide.

q: What is the primary symptom of 'Retinal Detachment'? / 'रेटिनल डिटैचमेंट' का प्राथमिक लक्षण क्या है?
o: Sudden flashes of light and floaters | प्रकाश की चमक और फ्लोटर्स
e: The sudden appearance of floaters or a 'curtain' over the vision indicates a detached retina.

q: Which malignant intraocular tumor is most common in children? / बच्चों में सबसे आम घातक इंट्राओकुलर ट्यूमर कौन सा है?
e: Retinoblastoma is a rare form of eye cancer that almost exclusively affects young children.

q: What is 'Hypopyon'? / 'हाइपोपियन' (Hypopyon) क्या है?
o: Pus in the anterior chamber | अग्र कक्ष में मवाद
e: It is a sign of severe inflammation or infection inside the eye (e.g., endophthalmitis).

q: Which part of the eye is affected in 'Uveitis'? / 'यूवेइिटिस' (Uveitis) में आँख का कौन सा हिस्सा प्रभावित होता है?
o: Uveal tract (Iris, Ciliary body, Choroid) | यूवेअल ट्रैक्ट
e: Uveitis refers to inflammation of the middle layer of the eye, which can lead to vision loss if untreated.

q: Which part of the eye is primarily responsible for the majority of 'Refraction'? / आँख का कौन सा हिस्सा मुख्य रूप से 'अपवर्तन' (Refraction) के अधिकांश भाग के लिए जिम्मेदार है?
e: The cornea provides approximately two-thirds of the eye's total optical power (about 43 diopters).

q: What is the clinical term for 'Nearsightedness'? / 'निकटदृष्टि दोष' के लिए नैदानिक शब्द क्या है?
e: In Myopia, light focuses in front of the retina, making distant objects appear blurry.

q: Which cranial nerve innervates the 'Superior Oblique' muscle? / कौन सी कपाल तंत्रिका 'सुपीरियर ऑब्लिक' मांसपेशी को नियंत्रित करती है?
e: The Trochlear nerve (IV) controls the Superior Oblique muscle (LR6SO4 rule).

q: What is the primary function of 'Rods' in the retina? / रेटिना में 'रॉड्स' (Rods) का प्राथमिक कार्य क्या है?
o: Vision in dim light (Scotopic vision) | मंद प्रकाश में दृष्टि
e: Rods are highly sensitive to light and are responsible for night vision but do not perceive color.

q: Which condition is characterized by an 'irregular curvature' of the cornea or lens? / कॉर्निया या लेंस की 'अनियमित वक्रता' की विशेषता क्या है?
e: Astigmatism causes light to focus on multiple points on the retina, leading to distorted vision.

q: The 'Aqueous Humor' is primarily produced by which structure? / 'एक्वियस ह्यूमर' का उत्पादन मुख्य रूप से किस संरचना द्वारा किया जाता है?
e: The ciliary processes of the ciliary body secrete aqueous humor into the posterior chamber.

q: What is 'Presbyopia'? / 'प्रेसबायोपिया' (Presbyopia) क्या है?
o: Age-related loss of near focusing ability | उम्र के साथ निकट दृष्टि की कमी
e: It occurs due to the gradual loss of elasticity in the lens, usually after age 40.

q: Which histological layer of the cornea is the thickest? / कॉर्निया की कौन सी हिस्टोलॉजिकल परत सबसे मोटी होती है?
e: The stroma makes up about 90% of the total corneal thickness.

q: What is the 'Optic Disc' also known as? / 'ऑप्टिक डिस्क' को और किस नाम से जाना जाता है?
e: The optic disc lacks photoreceptors, making it insensitive to light.

q: Which lens is used to correct 'Hyperopia' (Farsightedness)? / 'हाइपरोपिया' (दूरदृष्टि दोष) को ठीक करने के लिए किस लेंस का उपयोग किया जाता है?
e: Convex lenses converge light rays to focus them correctly on the retina in hyperopic eyes.

q: Which scale is used to assess the level of consciousness in a 'Coma' patient? / 'कोमा' रोगी में चेतना के स्तर का आकलन करने के लिए किस स्केल का उपयोग किया जाता है?
o: Glasgow Coma Scale (GCS) | ग्लासगो कोमा स्केल
e: GCS evaluates eye-opening, verbal, and motor responses to determine brain injury severity.

q: What is the underlying pathology in 'Parkinson's Disease'? / 'पार्किंसंस रोग' की अंतर्निहित पैथोफिज़ियोलॉजी क्या है?
o: Loss of Dopaminergic neurons in Substantia Nigra | डोपामाइन की कमी
e: The degeneration of dopamine-producing cells leads to tremors, rigidity, and bradykinesia.

q: A 'Cerebrovascular Accident' (Stroke) is primarily caused by: / 'सेरेब्रोवास्कुलर एक्सीडेंट' (स्ट्रोक) मुख्य रूप से किसके कारण होता है?
o: Ischemia or Hemorrhage in the brain | इस्किमिया या रक्तस्राव
e: A stroke occurs when blood supply to part of the brain is interrupted or reduced.

q: Which sign is a classic clinical indicator of 'Meningitis'? / 'मेनिन्जाइटिस' का क्लासिक नैदानिक संकेतक कौन सा है?
o: Nuchal rigidity (Stiff neck) | गर्दन में अकड़न
e: Meningeal irritation causes neck stiffness, fever, and altered mental status.

q: What is 'Myasthenia Gravis' primarily a disorder of? / 'मायस्थेनिया ग्रेविस' मुख्य रूप से किसका विकार है?
e: It is an autoimmune disease where antibodies block receptors at the junction between nerves and muscles.

q: Which of the following is a symptom of 'Hypothyroidism'? / निम्नलिखित में से कौन सा 'हाइपोथायरायडिज्म' का लक्षण है?
o: Weight gain and Cold intolerance | वजन बढ़ना और ठंड बर्दाश्त न होना
e: Low thyroid levels slow down metabolism, leading to fatigue and sensitivity to cold.

q: What is the diagnostic hallmark of 'Epilepsy'? / 'मिर्गी' (Epilepsy) की नैदानिक विशेषता क्या है?
o: Recurrent unprovoked seizures | बार-बार होने वाले दौरे
e: Epilepsy is defined by at least two unprovoked seizures occurring more than 24 hours apart.

q: In Rheumatic diseases, 'Rheumatoid Arthritis' typically affects: / संधिविज्ञान रोगों में, 'रुमेटॉइड अर्थराइटिस' आमतौर पर प्रभावित करता है:
o: Symmetrical small joints (Hands/Feet) | सममित छोटे जोड़
e: It is a chronic inflammatory disorder that usually targets the same joints on both sides of the body.

q: What is the primary metabolic complication of 'Obesity'? / 'मोटापा' की प्राथमिक चयापचय जटिलता क्या है?
o: Type 2 Diabetes and Dyslipidemia | टाइप 2 मधुमेह
e: Excess adipose tissue is a major risk factor for metabolic syndrome and cardiovascular disease.

q: The 'Encephalitis' is most commonly caused by: / 'एन्सेफलाइटिस' सबसे अधिक किसके कारण होता है?
e: Viral infections are the most frequent cause of acute inflammation of the brain tissue.

q: Which of the following is a classic clinical marker for Acute Myocardial Infarction (AMI)? / तीव्र मायोकार्डियल इंफार्क्शन (AMI) के लिए क्लासिक नैदानिक मार्कर कौन सा है?
o: Troponin I and T | ट्रोपोनिन I और T
e: Cardiac Troponins are highly specific and sensitive markers for myocardial injury, essential for diagnosing heart attacks.

q: What is the primary pathophysiology of 'Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus'? / 'टाइप 1 डायबिटीज मेलिटस' की प्राथमिक पैथोफिज़ियोलॉजी क्या है?
o: Autoimmune destruction of pancreatic beta cells | बीटा कोशिकाओं का विनाश
e: Type 1 diabetes results from the immune system attacking and destroying insulin-producing cells in the pancreas.

q: Which condition is characterized by 'Reed-Sternberg cells' on lymph node biopsy? / लिम्फ नोड बायोप्सी पर 'रीड-स्टर्नबर्ग कोशिकाओं' की विशेषता क्या है?
e: The presence of these large, multinucleated cells is diagnostic for Hodgkin's lymphoma.

q: In Nephrology, 'Nephrotic Syndrome' is primarily defined by: / नेफ्रोलॉजी में, 'नेफ्रोटिक सिंड्रोम' मुख्य रूप से किसके द्वारा परिभाषित किया गया है?
o: Massive proteinuria (>3.5g/day) and Edema | भारी प्रोटीनुरिया
e: Nephrotic syndrome involves high protein loss in urine, leading to low blood protein and severe body swelling.

q: Which genetic disorder is caused by an extra copy of chromosome 21? / क्रोमोसोम 21 की अतिरिक्त प्रति के कारण कौन सा आनुवंशिक विकार होता है?
e: Trisomy 21 leads to Down syndrome, characterized by intellectual disability and distinct physical features.

q: What is the hallmark of 'COPD' (Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease)? / 'COPD' की प्रमुख विशेषता क्या है?
o: Persistent and progressive airflow limitation | लगातार वायुप्रवाह सीमा
e: COPD involves chronic bronchitis and emphysema, leading to long-term breathing difficulties.

q: Which diagnostic finding is indicative of 'Liver Cirrhosis' on physical exam? / शारीरिक परीक्षण पर 'लिवर सिरोसिस' का कौन सा नैदानिक संकेत मिलता है?
e: Portal hypertension in cirrhosis causes fluid buildup in the abdomen (ascites) and an enlarged spleen.

q: What is the primary goal of 'Parenteral Nutrition'? / 'पैरेंट्रल पोषण' का प्राथमिक लक्ष्य क्या है?
o: Nutritional support via IV for non-functional GI tract | चतुर्थ (IV) पोषण
e: It provides essential nutrients directly into the bloodstream when the gut cannot be used.

q: Which joint is most commonly affected in 'Acute Gout'? / 'एक्यूट गाउट' में कौन सा जोड़ सबसे अधिक प्रभावित होता है?
o: First metatarsophalangeal joint (Big toe) | पैर का अंगूठा
e: Gout often presents as sudden, severe pain and swelling in the base of the big toe.

q: What is the primary treatment for 'Iron Deficiency Anemia'? / 'आयरन की कमी से होने वाले एनीमिया' का प्राथमिक उपचार क्या है?
o: Oral Iron supplementation | मौखिक आयरन सप्लीमेंट
e: Replacing iron stores is essential to restore normal hemoglobin levels and RBC production.

q: Which staging of Leprosy is characterized by high cell-mediated immunity and few lesions? / कुष्ठ रोग का कौन सा चरण उच्च कोशिका-मध्यस्थ प्रतिरक्षा और कम घावों द्वारा वर्गीकृत है?
e: Tuberculoid leprosy (TT) shows limited skin involvement because the host's immune response is strong.

q: What is the drug combination used in Multi-Drug Therapy (MDT) for 'Multibacillary' Leprosy? / 'मल्टीबैसिलरी' कुष्ठ रोग के लिए MDT में उपयोग किए जाने वाले दवाओं का संयोजन क्या है?
o: Rifampicin + Dapsone + Clofazimine | रिफैम्पिसिन + डैप्सोन + क्लोफैजि़माइन
e: MDT for MB leprosy includes three drugs to prevent antibiotic resistance and ensure cure.

q: Which STD is caused by 'Haemophilus ducreyi'? / 'हीमोफिलस डुक्रेयी' के कारण कौन सा एसटीडी होता है?
e: Chancroid presents as painful genital ulcers and regional lymphadenopathy (bubo).

q: What are 'Donovan Bodies' diagnostic of? / 'डोरोवन बॉडीज' (Donovan Bodies) किसके निदान के लिए उपयोग की जाती हैं?
e: Donovan bodies are seen in tissue smears from patients with Granuloma Inguinale.

q: Which ocular complication is common in Lepromatous Leprosy? / लेप्रोमेटस कुष्ठ रोग में कौन सी नेत्र संबंधी जटिलता आम है?
e: Nerve damage in leprosy can lead to the inability to close eyelids (lagophthalmos), risking blindness.

q: What is the primary diagnostic test for 'Gonorrhea'? / 'गोनोरिया' के लिए प्राथमिक नैदानिक परीक्षण क्या है?
o: Gram stain (Diplococci) and Culture | ग्राम स्टेन और कल्चर
e: Gram stain of urethral discharge showing Gram-negative intracellular diplococci is highly suggestive of Gonorrhea.

q: Which nerve is most frequently enlarged and palpable in Leprosy? / कुष्ठ रोग में कौन सी तंत्रिका सबसे अधिक बड़ी और स्पर्शनीय (palpable) हो जाती है?
e: The ulnar nerve at the elbow is the most common nerve involved in leprosy, often leading to claw hand.

q: What is the causative agent of 'Genital Herpes'? / 'जेनिटल हर्पीज' का कारक एजेंट क्या है?
e: HSV-2 is the most common cause of recurrent painful genital blisters.

q: In Leprosy, 'Leonine Facies' (lion-like face) is a feature of: / कुष्ठ रोग में 'लिओनाइन फेसीज' (शेर जैसा चेहरा) किसकी विशेषता है?
e: Extensive infiltration of the skin of the face leads to thickening and a lion-like appearance in advanced LL.

q: Which systemic involvement can lead to sterility in male Leprosy patients? / कुष्ठ रोग के पुरुष रोगियों में कौन सी प्रणालीगत संलिप्तता बांझपन का कारण बन सकती है?
e: Chronic orchitis in lepromatous leprosy can lead to testicular atrophy and sterility.

q: Which clinical sign is used to confirm Psoriasis by revealing pinpoint bleeding after scale removal? / सोरायसिस की पुष्टि करने के लिए किस नैदानिक संकेत का उपयोग किया जाता है?
e: Auspitz sign is characteristic of Psoriasis, where removing a scale reveals underlying pinpoint bleeding points.

q: What is the primary site of involvement in 'Pemphigus Vulgaris'? / 'पेम्फिगस वल्गेरिस' में संलिप्तता का प्राथमिक स्थल क्या है?
e: Pemphigus Vulgaris often starts with painful erosions in the mouth before spreading to the skin.

q: Which condition is a pre-malignant epidermal tumor often caused by chronic sun exposure? / क्रोनिक सन एक्सपोजर के कारण होने वाला प्री-मैलिग्नेंट एपिडर्मल ट्यूमर कौन सा है?
e: Actinic keratosis is a rough, scaly patch on the skin that can progress to squamous cell carcinoma.

q: What is the 'Koebner Phenomenon'? / 'कोबनेर घटना' (Koebner Phenomenon) क्या है?
o: New lesions appearing at sites of skin trauma | चोट वाली जगह पर नए घाव बनना
e: It is seen in Psoriasis and Lichen Planus where skin injury triggers new disease lesions at the site of trauma.

q: Which autoimmune rheumatologic disease is associated with 'Heliotrope rash' on the eyelids? / पलकों पर 'हेलियोट्रोप रैश' किस ऑटोइम्यून संधिविज्ञान रोग से जुड़ा है?
e: Dermatomyositis presents with a distinctive violet rash on the eyelids and muscle weakness.

q: What is 'Alopecia Areata'? / 'लोपेसिया एरीटा' (Alopecia Areata) क्या है?
o: Patchy hair loss due to autoimmune response | बालों का पैच में झड़ना
e: It is a condition where the immune system attacks hair follicles, causing sudden round patches of hair loss.

q: Which drug is most commonly associated with 'Toxic Epidermal Necrolysis' (TEN)? / 'टॉक्सिक एपिडर्मल नेक्रोलिसिस' (TEN) के साथ सबसे अधिक कौन सी दवा जुड़ी है?
e: TEN is a severe, life-threatening skin reaction often triggered by medications like sulfonamides or NSAIDs.

q: What is the characteristic feature of 'Lichen Planus'? / 'लिचेन प्लानस' (Lichen Planus) की विशिष्ट विशेषता क्या है?
o: Purple, pruritic, polygonal papules | बैंगनी और खुजलीदार पैप्युल्स
e: Lichen planus is known for the '6 Ps': Planar, Purple, Polygonal, Pruritic, Papules, and Plaques.

q: Which malignant tumor arises from melanocytes and has the highest mortality? / मेलानोसाइट्स से उत्पन्न होने वाला कौन सा घातक ट्यूमर सबसे अधिक मृत्यु दर वाला है?
e: Melanoma is the most dangerous form of skin cancer due to its high potential to metastasize.

q: What is 'Xeroderma Pigmentosum'? / 'जेरोडर्मा पिगमेंटोसम' (Xeroderma Pigmentosum) क्या है?
o: Genetic disorder with extreme sensitivity to UV rays | यूवी किरणों के प्रति संवेदनशीलता
e: It is a rare condition where the skin cannot repair damage caused by ultraviolet light, leading to early skin cancers.

q: Which bacteria is the primary causative agent of 'Syphilis'? / 'सिफलिस' का प्राथमिक कारक एजेंट कौन सा बैक्टीरिया है?
e: Syphilis is a chronic bacterial infection transmitted through sexual contact.

q: What is the hallmark of 'Secondary Syphilis'? / 'द्वितीयक सिफलिस' की प्रमुख विशेषता क्या है?
e: The second stage involves a widespread rash, often including the palms and soles.

q: Which fungal infection is common in the genital area (Fungal STD)? / जननांग क्षेत्र में कौन सा फंगल संक्रमण आम है (Fungal STD)?
e: Candidiasis is a yeast infection that can cause itching and discharge in both men and women.

q: What causes 'Molluscum Contagiosum'? / 'मोलस्कम कोंटागियोसम' किसके कारण होता है?
e: It is a viral skin infection presenting as small, firm, umbilicated papules.

q: What is the causative organism of 'Leprosy'? / 'कुष्ठ रोग' (Leprosy) का कारक जीव क्या है?
e: M. leprae is an acid-fast bacillus that primarily affects nerves and skin.

q: Which cardinal sign is essential for diagnosing Leprosy? / कुष्ठ रोग के निदान के लिए कौन सा प्रमुख संकेत अनिवार्य है?
o: Loss of sensation in skin patches | त्वचा के धब्बों में संवेदनहीनता
e: Loss of touch, pain, or temperature sensation in a skin lesion is a definitive sign of leprosy.

q: What is 'Type 1 Reaction' in Leprosy also known as? / कुष्ठ रोग में 'टाइप 1 रिएक्शन' को और किस नाम से जाना जाता है?
e: Type 1 reaction is a delayed hypersensitivity response causing acute inflammation of existing lesions.

q: Which STD is characterized by a 'Painful' ulcer (Chancroid)? / 'दर्दनाक' अल्सर (चैनक्रॉइड) किस एसटीडी की विशेषता है?
e: Unlike the painless chancre of syphilis, chancroid ulcers are very painful.

q: What is 'Erythema Nodosum Leprosum' (ENL)? / 'एरिथेमा नोडोसम लेप्रोसम' (ENL) क्या है?
e: ENL is a systemic immune-complex mediated reaction occurring in lepromatous patients.

q: Which viral STD is caused by 'Human Papilloma Virus' (HPV)? / 'ह्यूमन पैपिलोमा वायरस' (HPV) के कारण कौन सा वायरल एसटीडी होता है?
e: Certain types of HPV cause warts, while others are linked to cervical cancer.

q: Skin manifestations like 'Butterfly Rash' are typical of which systemic disease? / 'बटरफ्लाई रैश' जैसे त्वचा के लक्षण किस प्रणालीगत रोग के विशिष्ट हैं?
e: Malar rash (butterfly rash) is a key diagnostic feature of SLE, a rheumatologic disease.

q: What is 'Acanthosis Nigricans' often a skin manifestation of? / 'एकैंथोसिस निग्रिकन्स' अक्सर किसका त्वचा संबंधी लक्षण होता है?
e: Dark, velvety patches in body folds are associated with metabolic conditions and internal malignancies.

q: Which epidermal appendageal disorder is commonly known as 'Acne'? / कौन सा एपिडर्मल उपांग विकार आमतौर पर 'मुँहासे' (Acne) के रूप में जाना जाता है?
e: Acne affects the pilosebaceous unit, including hair follicles and sebaceous glands.

q: Which physical factor causes 'Chilblains' on the skin? / त्वचा पर 'चिलब्लेन्स' किस भौतिक कारक के कारण होता है?
o: Cold and damp weather | ठंड और नम मौसम
e: Chilblains are painful inflammations of small blood vessels in response to cold exposure.

q: A 'Fixed Drug Eruption' (FDE) typically recurs at: / एक 'फिक्स्ड ड्रग इरप्शन' (FDE) आमतौर पर कहाँ दोबारा होता है?
o: The same site as previous eruption | पिछले स्थान पर ही
e: FDE is a type of drug reaction that reappears at exactly the same location upon re-exposure.

q: What is 'Urticaria' commonly called? / 'अर्टिकेरिया' (Urticaria) को आमतौर पर क्या कहा जाता है?
e: Urticaria presents as itchy, raised wheals often due to an allergic reaction to drugs or food.

q: Which neoplastic disorder of the dermis is associated with HIV/AIDS? / डर्मिस का कौन सा नियोप्लास्टिक विकार HIV/AIDS से जुड़ा है?
e: Kaposi sarcoma is a vascular tumor caused by HHV-8, frequently seen in immunocompromised patients.
